string.text: Revert every AlifePlus setting to its factory default. Takes effect immediately -- close and re-open MCM to see the updated sliders. Useful after experimenting or when upgrading to a new version with rebalanced defaults.
Event pipeline controls. Events pass through specialized rate limiters controlled by A-Life Ratio, A-Life Interval, Cause Budget, Consequence Budget.

Balance between events on the player's map and background maps, similar to X-Ray's original going speed. Higher values favor the player's map. Zero treats all maps equally. Negative values favor background maps.

[seconds]\nTime between radiant event evaluations. Lower means more activity, higher means a calmer Zone. Direct reactive events use a separate limiter with a fixed interval per event type, to accommodate bursty events like deaths.
Max reaction publishes per 60s (massacre, squadkill, basekill, wounded, harvest, alpha, alphakill).
Max stalker-needs publishes per 60s (hunger, sleep, rest, heal, shelter, money, supply, job, social).
Max mutant-instinct publishes per 60s (scatter, feed, sleep, explore, socialize, infest).
Max territorial-opportunity publishes per 60s (stash interactions, area conquest).
[count]\nMax activations per consequence type per 60 seconds. Prevents one consequence from firing too often.
[count]\nMax total consequence activations across all types per 60 seconds. Prevents consequence floods from multiple causes firing in quick succession.

When enabled, other mods can register squad ownership. AP will not touch squads owned by other mods (Warfare, BAO, etc). When disabled, AP ignores external ownership claims and controls all eligible squads.

PDA gossip composer. Zone events (kills, conquests, raids, mutant activity) are recorded as they happen, then periodically composed into radio chatter and delivered via PDA by an event-connected stalker.
Master toggle for the PDA news composer. When disabled, no gossip is dispatched. Event recording continues regardless.
[seconds]\nMinimum delay between PDA messages. Each tick picks a random delay between min and max, so cadence feels human instead of mechanical.
[seconds]\nMaximum delay between PDA messages. Higher means a quieter Zone overall. Set min and max to the same value for fixed cadence.

Which faction radios you tune into. Own: only your faction speaks. Allies: your faction plus factions friendly to you. World: any non-enemy stalker. Secret faction channels (Monolith, Army, Greh, ISG) stay member-only regardless. Default: allies.
[game hours]\nHow old an event can be and still get gossiped about. Each compose tick picks a random event from the past N game hours. Lower = fresher news, higher = wider gossip pool. Default: 12.
Mutant accumulated enough kills to level up as an alpha. 10 levels, each requiring more kills. Stalkers use engine rank instead.
Fire cause:alpha when a mutant reaches a new alpha level.
Kills required per alpha level. At 2: level 1 at 2 kills, level 10 at 20 kills.
Fire cause:alphakill event when alpha mutants die. Required for targeted pack hunt.
Deaths pile up at a smart terrain.
Track death accumulation and fire massacre events when threshold reached.
Deaths required in area before massacre event fires.
[seconds]\nTime window for death accumulation. Deaths older than this don't count.
Allow the stash generator to publish cause:stash_full.
Allow the stash generator to publish cause:stash_trap.
Allow the stash generator to publish cause:stash_empty.
Game hours per squad before another loot attempt. MVT recovery time.
Game hours per outlaw squad between ambush stakeouts.
Game hours per squad between attempts to seed an empty stash.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
Minimum item count for outlaws to stake out a stash. Below this, they loot directly instead.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
A squad has been wiped out.
Fire cause:squadkill event when last squad member dies.
Deaths pile up at a faction base.
Track deaths at faction bases and fire events when threshold reached.
Deaths required at base before event fires.
[seconds]\nTime window for death accumulation at base.
An NPC or player used a healing item.
Fire event when NPC or player uses healing item.
Seconds before the same smart terrain can trigger area cause again.
Allow the area generator to publish cause:area_conquer.
Game hours per squad before another conquer attempt. MVT recovery time.
An NPC or player picked up an artefact.
Fire cause:harvest when artefact is picked up.

Squads develop human needs over time (hunger, sleep, rest, healing, shelter, money, supplies, duty, social). Hull drive scoring picks the strongest unmet need. Thresholds are game-hours between need events.
Enable hunger need. Squads seek campfires to eat when overdue.
Enable sleep need. Dormant-period only (night for stalkers). Squads seek campfires to sleep.
Enable rest need. Squads seek campfires to smoke, drink, or relax.
Enable heal need. Squads return to friendly bases for medical attention.
Enable shelter need. Squads seek non-enemy bases for environmental protection.
Enable money need. Squads visit anomaly zones or mutant lairs for resources.
Enable supply need. Squads visit traders for resupply.
Enable job need. Squads guard, explore, or research based on faction.
Enable social need. Squads gather at campfires or bases for human connection.
[game-hours]\nTime between rest events (smoke, drink, relax). Default 4.
[game-hours]\nTime between supply events (trader visit, stash deposit). Default 48.
[game-hours]\nTime between job events (guard, explore, research). Default 18.
[game-hours]\nTime between social events (campfire gathering, base visit). Default 36.
When the Alpha cause fires, registers the mutant as alpha. Damage modifiers configured in Mutators section.
Register mutants as alphas when they level up from kills.
Maximum number of alphas allowed worldwide. Only blocks new alphas, not level-ups.
Alpha mutants carry valuable items (mutant parts, hides, artefacts). Better loot at higher levels. Items persist in inventory and drop on death.
When an alpha mutant dies, same-species mutants on the same level hunt the killer.
Same-species mutants on the level pursue the killer.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum alpha squads that pursue the killer.
Maximum times a hunter re-scripts to follow a fleeing killer. 0 disables chase.
Cause: massacre\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: scent range
When the Massacre cause fires, nearby outlaws and cowardly mutants (flesh, zombie, rats) converge on the site.
Outlaws and cowardly mutants converge on massacre sites.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum scavenger squads that respond to a massacre.
Cause: massacre\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When the Massacre cause fires, the victims' faction sends nearby squads to investigate the site.
Stalker factions investigate when their own die.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum faction squads that investigate a massacre (random 1..N).
Cause: stash\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: eye range
When the Stash cause fires, the discovering squad (outlaws) camps at the stash location. They don't touch the stash, it's bait.
Bandits camp at revealed stash locations and wait.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Cause: stash\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: eye range
When the Stash cause fires, the discovering stalker squad moves to loot the stash. What's inside is unknown until arrival.
Scavengers race to revealed stashes to loot them before player arrives.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
When enabled, NPCs skip stashes containing toolkits, repair kits, craft tools, or workshop kits. The entire stash is left untouched.
When the Stash cause fires, the discovering stalker squad moves to hide supplies in the stash. What's inside is unknown until arrival.
Stalkers hide supplies in stashes they discover.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Minimum items to hide in stash.
Maximum items to hide in stash.
Cause: squadkill\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When the SquadKill cause fires, nearby same-faction squads pursue the killer (NPC or player) organically through the simulation as it moves.
Nearby friendly squads pursue killers when squad is wiped.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum avenger squads that pursue a killer (random 1..N).
Maximum times an avenger re-scripts to follow a fleeing killer. 0 disables chase.
Cause: squadkill\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When the SquadKill cause fires, nearby squads of the victim's faction flee to the nearest faction base.
Squads retreat to safety when nearby friendlies are eliminated.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum squads that flee per event (random 1..N).
Cause: basekill\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When the BaseKill cause fires, nearby friendly squads rush to reinforce the base under attack.
Nearby squads rush to reinforce base under attack.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum reinforcement squads sent to defend base (random 1..N).
Cause: basekill\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: eye range
When the BaseKill cause fires, squads at the base evacuate to the nearest faction base.
Squads abandon base under heavy attack and retreat to safety.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum squads that flee per event (random 1..N).
Cause: wounded\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When the Wounded cause fires, nearby predator and aberrant mutants move toward the wounded NPC or player.
Predator and aberrant mutants hunt wounded.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum mutant squads drawn to wounded (random 1..N).
Cause: wounded\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When the Wounded cause fires, nearby same-faction squads rush to help the wounded NPC or player.
Faction members rush to assist wounded comrades.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum helper squads sent per event (random 1..N).
Cause: area_conquer\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: eye range
Human factions (alignment_human minus ecolog) claim empty smart terrains. The conqueror's faction takes ownership and the engine spawns their squads there.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Enable persistent area ownership via respawn mutation.
Maximum smart terrains that can be conquered. FIFO eviction when reached.
Extra squads at conquered area. Added alongside original spawns.
Conquered smart terrains lose the conqueror's spawns after this many game hours. Original spawns are unaffected. Set to 0 to disable decay.
Conquered areas spawn the conqueror's squads alongside original spawns. Disable to keep squad movement without spawn changes.
Cause: area_conquer\nRules: species, personality\nEval: eye range
Feral, predator, and aberrant mutants claim empty smart terrains. The conqueror's species takes ownership and the engine spawns their squads there.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Cause: harvest\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When the Harvest cause fires, nearby outlaws pursue the NPC or player who picked up the artefact.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum hunter squads that pursue the artefact taker (random 1..N).
Maximum times a hunter re-scripts to follow a fleeing taker. 0 disables chase.
Cause: harvest\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: scent range
When the Harvest cause fires, nearby aberrant mutants converge on the artefact pickup site.
Aberrant mutants converge on artefact pickup sites.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Maximum aberrant squads that converge on the pickup site (random 1..N).
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When hunger fires, squad moves to a campfire smart to eat and drink.
Hungry stalkers seek campfires to eat.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC eats food from inventory: bread, sausage, canned goods, vodka, beer. Item is removed.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When sleep fires, squad moves to a campfire smart to sleep. Night-gated by cause (20:00-05:00).
Tired stalkers seek campfires to sleep at night.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When rest fires, squad moves to a campfire smart to relax, smoke, or drink.
Fatigued stalkers seek campfires to rest.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC smokes a cigarette or drinks vodka/beer from inventory. Item is removed.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When heal fires, squad moves to a sheltered location for medical attention.
Wounded stalkers seek sheltered locations for healing.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC uses a medkit, bandage, or stimpack from inventory. Item is removed.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When shelter fires, squad moves to a surge-sheltered location for protection.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When no indoor shelter is available, squad moves to a campfire for outdoor cover.
Exposed stalkers seek campfires when no surge shelter is nearby.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When money fires, squad moves to an anomaly zone to harvest artefacts.
Stalkers seek anomaly zones when they need money.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When money fires, squad moves to a mutant lair to hunt for trophies and pelts.
Stalkers hunt mutants when they need money.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When supply fires, squad moves to a trader smart to exchange goods.
Stalkers visit traders when they need supplies.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC sells one artefact from inventory and receives ammo, grenades, or medical supplies. Both items are real.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When job fires, squad moves to a friendly outpost or checkpoint for guard duty.
Stalkers guard outposts and checkpoints when duty calls.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC smokes a cigarette from inventory while on guard duty. Item is removed.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When job fires, squad moves to a distant or unvisited smart terrain to explore.
Stalkers explore unknown areas when duty calls.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When job fires, squad moves to an anomaly zone to study anomalous phenomena.
Stalkers research anomaly zones when duty calls.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When social fires, squad moves to a campfire smart to play guitar, tell stories, or socialize.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC shares a cigarette or drink from inventory at the campfire. Item is removed.
Cause: needs\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: radio range
When social fires, squad moves to own-faction base for companionship and morale.
Lonely stalkers return to base for company.
Scripted squads move at maximum speed. Disable for slower, more realistic movement.
On arrival, NPC shares a cigarette or drink from inventory at base. Item is removed.
Combat modifiers for alpha mutants. Alphas are mutants that earned their status through kills. Hit power bonus makes their attacks land harder. Hit power reduction makes incoming hits land softer.
Alpha mutant attacks land harder. Scales with alpha level.
Hit power bonus per alpha level. At 0.05: level 1 = +5%, level 10 = +50%.
Incoming hits against alpha mutants land softer. Scales with alpha level.
Hit power reduction per alpha level. At 0.05: level 1 = -5%, level 10 = -50%.
Alpha mutants never panic or flee. Panic threshold set to zero on first hit.
Combat modifiers for veteran+ stalkers (rank 12000+). Based on engine rank earned through kills. Scales linearly from veteran to legend.
Veteran+ stalker attacks land harder. A legend at max bonus hits significantly harder.
Maximum hit power bonus at legend rank. At 0.15: veteran = +3%, legend = +15%.
Incoming hits against veteran+ stalkers land softer. A legend at max reduction takes significantly less.
Maximum hit power reduction at legend rank. At 0.15: veteran = -3%, legend = -15%.
Debug and development. DEBUG enables tracing, performance timing, and verbose logs. Debug markers show squad destinations on the PDA map.
Controls log verbosity and debug features. DEBUG enables OTel-style tracing, performance timing, and verbose logs. Has performance cost.
Show squad destination markers on the PDA map. Approximate positions for tracing A-Life movement, not exact. Reload save to apply.
Show pipeline statistics on screen and write to log every 10 seconds. Select position or Off. Zero overhead when disabled.

Mutant squads develop instincts over time (feed, sleep, explore, socialize). Hull drive scoring picks the strongest unmet instinct. Day/night cycle: nocturnal species (bloodsuckers, lurkers, chimeras, zombies, fractures) are active at night and sleep during the day. All other species are active during the day. Thresholds are game-hours between instinct events.
Enable scatter instinct. Prey and lower-tier mutants scatter when they see a higher-tier predator within eye range. No time threshold -- binary threat detection.
Enable feed instinct. Mutants move to open territory to hunt or scavenge during active hours.

Enable hibernate instinct. Mutants return to species-appropriate shelter during dormant hours. Cowardly mutants hibernate in open fields. Feral mutants hibernate in lairs. Predators hibernate in lairs or buildings. Aberrant mutants hibernate in buildings.
Enable explore instinct. Mutants wander to nearby territory or lair during active hours.
Enable socialize instinct. Mutants move toward smart terrains where same-faction squads are present.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the feed personality roll. Even species with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Traits: SURVIVAL + PERCEPTION. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the feed personality roll. Even species with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the hibernate personality roll. Even species with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Trait: SURVIVAL. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the hibernate personality roll. Even species with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
Cause: instincts\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: eye range
Prey and lower-tier mutants scatter when they see a higher-tier predator within eye range. Food chain: cowardly fears feral/predator/aberrant, feral fears predator/aberrant, predator fears aberrant.
Route lower-tier mutants away from nearby higher-tier predators to a safe smart terrain.
Mutants sprint when scattering from predators.
Mutants move to open territory during active hours to hunt or scavenge. Predators and prey meet on shared hunting grounds.
Route mutants to open territory for feeding.
Mutants return to species-appropriate rest locations during dormant hours. Cowardly hibernate in fields, feral in lairs, predators in lairs or buildings, aberrant underground.
Route mutants to species-appropriate rest locations.
Cause: instincts\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: scent range
Mutants wander to a different territory or lair during active hours. Aberrant excluded (lair-bound).
Route mutants to nearby territory or lair for exploration.
Cause: instincts\nRules: alignment, personality\nEval: scent range
Pack animals move toward smart terrains where same-faction squads are present. Predator and aberrant excluded (solitary).
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even species with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even species with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the scatter personality roll. Traits: INV_AGGRESSION + SURVIVAL. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the scatter personality roll. Traits: INV_AGGRESSION + SURVIVAL. Default: 0.60.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits will pass at least this often. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.

Mutants claim smart terrains as nests. Exclusive spawn injection replaces original spawns with the infesting species. Feral, predator, and aberrant species only (cowardly excluded). Per-level cap limits how many smarts can be infested on each level.
Enable the area infest consequence. Mutants will claim smart terrains as nests.
When enabled, squads move at rush speed to claim their nest.
[0.0-1.0]\nMinimum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with low matching traits have at least this chance. Default: 0.20.
[0.0-1.0]\nMaximum chance for the personality roll. Even factions with high matching traits are capped at this. Default: 0.70.
Apply exclusive spawn injection on infested smarts. Replaces original spawns with the infesting species.
[1-5]\nMaximum number of infested smart terrains per level. Default: 1.
[1-5]\nNumber of squads the infested spawn entry can produce. Default: 3.
[0-168]\nGame hours before infestation expires. 0 = permanent (no decay). Default: 0.
Enable the area infest cause. Mutants will seek to claim smart terrains as nests.
[0.1-168]\nGame hours per squad before another infest attempt. MVT recovery time. Default: 24.
